name: Group Stage Finale Into the Last 32
angle: The 2026 group stage closes out: Brazil and Mexico finish with perfect intent, South Africa go through, Ronaldo writes another record line, and the 32 names for the knockouts take shape.

questions.question: Brazil sealed top spot in Group C on Wednesday with a 3-0 win over which side, leaving their World Cup hopes hanging by a thread?
Which Real Madrid forward scored both of Brazil's first two goals in the 3-0 win over Scotland?
Who came off the Brazil bench against Scotland to make his first appearance for the national team in roughly three years?
Tournament debutants Cape Verde produced one of the World Cup's great shocks by holding European champions Spain to a 0-0 draw. Which goalkeeper kept the clean sheet?
Mexico beat Czechia 3-0 at the Azteca to achieve which feat for the first time in their long World Cup history?

options.B: Keep three clean sheets in a group

questions.question: Coming off the bench against Czechia, 40-year-old Guillermo Ochoa featured at his sixth World Cup, joining which two players as the only men to appear at six?
A 1-0 win over South Korea booked which nation's place in the round of 32?
South Korea's hopes faded after head coach Hong Myung-bo made the controversial call to leave which captain out of his starting line-up?
Cristiano Ronaldo's brace in Portugal's 5-0 win over Uzbekistan made him the first player ever to score at how many different World Cups?
Ronaldo, scoring at 41, became the second-oldest goalscorer in World Cup history. Who remains the oldest, having struck for Cameroon at USA 94?
Croatia ground out a 1-0 win over Panama, the only goal coming from which striker?
Colombia edged DR Congo 1-0 thanks to a second goal in two games from which marauding full-back?
Morocco beat Haiti 4-2 but finished second in Group C, level on seven points with Brazil. What edged them out of top spot?
This is the first World Cup with 48 teams and a round of 32. How many of the third-placed sides across the 12 groups advance to that round?
Vinicius became only the fifth Brazilian to score in all three group matches at a World Cup. Which forward set the benchmark by scoring in every match of Brazil's victorious 1970 campaign?

sources: https://www.espn.com/soccer/story/_/id/49167442/scotland-0-3-brazil-vinicius-junior-leaves-tartan-army-2026-world-cup-hopes-serious-jeopardy-miami (Scotland 0-3 Brazil, June 25 — Vinicius brace, Cunha, Brazil top Group C)

https://www.espn.com/soccer/story/_/id/49169585/vinicius-junior-brazil-scotland-fifa-2026-world-cup-knockout (Vinicius the fifth Brazilian to score in all three group games, joining Jairzinho 1970, Romario 1994, Ronaldo and Rivaldo 2002)

https://www.sportsmole.co.uk/football/scotland/world-cup-2026/feature/vinicius-junior-seals-group-c-top-spot-as-neymar-marks-long-awaited-return_600025.html (Neymar's first Brazil minutes in 918 days off the bench)
https://www.espn.com/soccer/story/_/id/49171513/guillermo-ochoa-cameo-seals-perfect-mexico-group-stage (Ochoa sixth-WC cameo; Mexico 3-0 Czechia at the Azteca; first perfect group stage)
https://www.nbcnews.com/sports/soccer/mexico-routs-czechia-win-3-world-cup-group-matches-1st-time-rcna351702 (Mexico win all three group games for the first time in their history)
https://www.espn.com/soccer/story/_/id/49172732/world-cup-2026-today-blog-25-06-2026 (June 25 live blog — South Africa 1-0 South Korea, Son benched by Hong Myung-bo; Morocco 4-2 Haiti)
https://www.aljazeera.com/sports/2026/6/23/ronaldo-scores-twice-as-portugal-thrash-uzbekistan-5-0-at-world-cup-2026 (Ronaldo first to score at six different World Cups; Portugal 5-0 Uzbekistan)
https://www.espn.com/soccer/story/_/id/49153993/portugal-uzbekistan-live-world-cup-2026 (Ronaldo, 41, second-oldest WC scorer behind Roger Milla)

https://www.espn.in/espn/story/_/id/49160160/2026-fifa-world-cup-recap-portugal-uzbekistan-ghana-england-croatia-panama-colombia-congo-dr (Day 13 recap — Croatia 1-0 Panama Budimir; Colombia 1-0 DR Congo Munoz)
https://www.espn.com/soccer/match/_/gameId/760428/cape-verde-spain (Spain 0-0 Cape Verde, June 15 — debutants hold the European champions; Vozinha in goal)
